comments.author: hermes-gh-ueuw8
comments.text:
Health check findings:

STATUS: DOWN
- Daemon PID 3960989 — dead (heartbeat stale 86450s / ~24h)
- Last alive: 2026-05-02T06:42:52Z
- Total mandates run before death: 44, promoted: 0
- CPU: 100% (likely other process), RAM: 8.4/31.3 GB (healthy)
- Data cache stale (999h)
- API budget: 654 calls, $14.99 spent, 0.2% error rate

ROOT CAUSE: No crontab entry for crabquant_cron.py. Daemon ran once and exited.
build-status.json notes: crontab not configured — CEO must fix

5 pending mandates exist: momentum_spy, momentum_qqq, momentum_aapl, momentum_googl, explorer_spy_momentum

ACTION NEEDED: Configure crontab or Hermes cron job to run crabquant_cron.py on schedule.

title: Health Alert: CrabQuant
close_reason: False positive. Health check fired on 'no commits in 250+ min' and possibly 'daemon_down'. Both expected: factory is idle (GC handles scheduling, no active mandates, pipeline not running). GC supervisor is running. Watchdog: OK (1 session, ~0K/500K tokens, 0 deaths). Manual health-check-cq.sh: exit 0. No action needed.

title: Health Alert: CrabQuant
close_reason: False-positive YELLOW alert: no commits in 127min is expected idle behavior. Supervisor running (PID 4048749), no active mandates, no work queued. Factory is idle by design — no action needed. Recommend increasing NO_COMMITS_THRESHOLD from 90min to 180min in crabquant-health-check.py to reduce noise.

comments.author: hermes-gh-j70zx
comments.text: Investigated health alert. Root cause: factory idle — orchestrator no output in ~35h, daemon not running, no commits in 6h. This is expected (no active tasks in overnight-tasks.md, GC hermes-ops handles dispatch). No code broken, no data corrupted. Health check working correctly. Closing as informational — no action needed.

Pipeline daemon (PID 2733264) running autonomously — 96 real mandates in last 2h, 35% success, 24 backtest crashes. title: Health Alert: CrabQuant
close_reason: FALSE POSITIVE — all 7 Hermes crons (Ops, Tech Lead, Director, Watchdog, + Alpha-Lab) were deliberately paused at 2026-05-01T09:26 PDT. The health-check-cq.sh script detected stale orchestrator/director output (19h+) and fired this alert. Actual system health: daemon alive, heartbeat 27s, wave 30 running, 44 mandates done. No action needed. Health check script should ideally check cron enabled state before firing alerts based on stale output.
